mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent signals severe labor market slack and depressed consumer spending, which forces earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of instances when unemployment exceeds 8.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean return over 60 trading days, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates manufacturing contraction and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows and negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Short-term 5-day returns remain positive or flat following industrial production declines, or the signal shows no regime-dependent predictive power in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following inflation spikes above 0.3 percent, or the inflation threshold fails to separate positive and negative return regimes with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative or zero alpha over 20 trading days, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from independent series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent indicates severe economic stagnation, prompting earnings recession fears and capital flight from equities, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Markets sustain positive returns over 60 days following sub-1.0 percent GDP prints, or the threshold fails to predict return direction in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and retail sales validates a synchronized recovery in manufacturing and consumption, reducing recession probabilities and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The synchronized supply-demand signal fails to outperform the market over 20 trading days, or the joint effect loses predictive accuracy when tested across multiple recovery cycles.
